title: STANDARD FOR CRACKERS FROM MARINE AND FRESHWATER FISH, CRUSTACEAN AND MOLLUSCAN SHELLFISH

sections.section: STANDARD FOR CRACKERS FROM MARINE AND FRESHWATER FISH, CRUSTACEAN AND MOLLUSCAN SHELLFISH
sections.summary: Document title: Standard for crackers made from marine and freshwater fish, crustacean and molluscan shellfish.
Document identifier: CXS 222-2001.
Adoption and amendment history: adopted in 2001 and amended in 2011, 2013, 2016 and 2018.
Scope: applies to crackers prepared from marine and freshwater fish, crustacean and molluscan shellfish but excludes ready-to-eat fried and artificially flavoured crackers.

Description: the product is made from fresh or frozen minced marine/freshwater fish, crustacean and molluscan shellfish, prepared by mixing, forming, cooking, cooling, slicing and drying, packed in moisture-proof, gas-impermeable material to minimize oxidation, and raw materials must be chilled/iced to 0°C promptly and kept at adequate temperatures prior to processing.

Product definition: crackers are a traditional food made from fresh or frozen minced marine or freshwater fish, crustacean (including prawns/shrimps) and molluscan shellfish (including squid, cuttlefish, oysters, clams, mussels, cockles) plus other ingredients described elsewhere.
Process definition: product shall be prepared by mixing, forming, cooking, cooling, slicing and drying and packed in moisture-proof, gas-impermeable packaging processed to minimize oxidation.

Handling practice: fresh marine/freshwater fish, crustacean and molluscan shellfish must be chilled or iced immediately after harvest to 0°C as quickly as possible and maintained at adequate temperatures to prevent spoilage and bacterial growth (per CXC 52-2003).
Section heading: Essential composition and quality factors (contains subsections on raw material, other ingredients, optional ingredients and final product requirements and a compliance table).

Raw material definition: fresh marine and freshwater fish, crustacean and molluscan shellfish mean freshly caught, chilled or frozen products (including appropriately processed frozen minced flesh) with characteristic fresh appearance, colour and odour.
Other ingredients requirement: all other ingredients must be food-grade quality and conform to applicable Codex Standards.
Optional ingredients: the product may contain sugar and suitable spices.

Final product requirements: crackers must have uniform size, shape, colour, thickness and texture and comply with the specification table (e.g., minimum crude protein and specified moisture content ranges by grade).

Permits the use of flavour enhancers and sequestrants for foods in Codex food category 09.2.5 (smoked, dried, fermented and/or salted fish and fish products, including mollusks, crustaceans and echinoderms) and its parent categories when used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995).

Recommends preparing and handling the product according to the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Practice for Fish and Fishery Products (CXC 52-2003) and other relevant codes, and requires compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.

Adds labelling obligations beyond the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), including prescribed product names, grade declaration per Table 1 when used, and clear storage and cooking directions on the package.

Requires products from marine and freshwater fish to be named "Fish Crackers" and products from crustacean or molluscan shellfish to show the common species name (e.g., "Prawn Crackers", "Squid Crackers").
States that when grade is declared the package must show the grade as prescribed in Table 1.
Requires the package to bear clear instructions for keeping the product from purchase until use and directions for cooking.

Specifies sampling and analysis requirements: sampling of lots per an appropriate plan with an AQL of 6.5; crude protein and moisture determined by specified AOAC methods; sensory and physical examinations by trained assessors per Annex A; and refers to Section 8 for definitions of defectives (foreign matter, odour/flavour, bones, discolouration).
Requires lot sampling to follow an appropriate sampling plan with an acceptance quality limit (AQL) of 6.5.
Specifies crude protein be determined according to AOAC methods 920.87 or 960.52.
Specifies moisture determination be performed according to AOAC 950.46B (air drying).
Requires sensory and physical examination samples to be assessed by trained persons in accordance with Annex A.
Defines a sample unit as defective if it exhibits any of the specified defects in Section 8, including foreign matter, objectionable odour or flavour, excessive bones, or pronounced discolouration.

Defines foreign matter as any material not derived from the specified ingredients that is readily recognizable without magnification, does not pose a health threat, and indicates non‑compliance with good manufacturing and sanitation practices.

Declares as defective unfried crackers with persistent, distinct objectionable odours and fried crackers with persistent, distinct objectionable flavours indicative of decomposition or contamination (e.g., fuel oil, cleaning compounds).
Counts crackers as defective if they contain more than one bone exceeding 3 mm in diameter and 5 mm in length that affects more than 25% of the sample unit.
Considers pronounced black, whitish or yellowish surface discolouration indicative of mould or fungal growth that affects more than 10% of the sample unit to be a defect.

Specifies lot acceptance criteria: total defectives must not exceed the acceptance number (c) from a sampling plan with AQL 6.5; average net weight must be at least the declared weight with no individual container under 95% of declared weight; and Sections 4, 5 and 6 (Food Additives, Hygiene, Labelling) must be met.

Provides sensory/physical examination procedures: use a different sample for sensory evaluation, inspect for foreign matter, bones and discolouration, assess uncooked odour and cooked flavour per CXG 31-1999, and deep‑fry samples in fresh oil at 190°C for 20–60 seconds as appropriate to cracker thickness.
Non‑substantive heading only (Annex A) with no text content provided.
